title: Privacy of Groups in Dense Street Imagery
source: Franchi, Sandhaus, Choksi, Engelmann, Ju & Nissenbaum · ACM FAccT 2025
blurb: When the sidewalk is photographed at scale, it isn't only individuals who are captured but whole groups — and group privacy is exactly what conventional, person-by-person protections miss.

title: Detecting disparities in police deployments using dashcam data
source: Franchi, Zamfirescu-Pereira, Ju & Pierson · ACM FAccT 2023
blurb: A fleet of rideshare dashcams becomes a city-scale sensing network — here, to measure where police are deployed across New York and surface the disparities in who gets watched.

title: Nexar dashcam video database hacked
blurb: A breach exposed 130+ TB of dashcam footage from Nexar's cloud — a concrete reminder that the dashcam layer in this map is a real, breachable archive of faces and places.

title: Bill requiring smart glasses to show a recording indicator
source: Rep. Joe Ciresi (H.B. 2603) · PA House · June 2026
blurb: Pennsylvania moves to mandate a visible light whenever smart glasses record — a direct policy response to the covert-capture problem the "In 5 years…" glasses layer imagines.

title: Meta's Ray-Ban glasses users film and harass massage-parlor workers
blurb: Instagram accounts with huge followings repost covert Ray-Ban-glasses footage of people at massage parlors — the smart-glasses layer's harm, already here, not five years out.
